overview: A single Go-based RESTful backend service exposes versioned CRUD endpoints for todo items, persisting data to a relational database. The service handles validation, pagination, filtering, sorting, health/readiness checks, and graceful shutdown, communicating with clients over JSON/HTTP.
requirements: CRUD operations on todo items via /v1 endpoints
Fields: id, title (required, ≤200 chars), description (≤2000 chars), completion status, due date (ISO 8601), priority (low/medium/high), created_at, updated_at
List endpoint supports pagination (limit/offset), filtering by completion/priority/due-date range, sorting by created_at/due_date/priority asc/desc
Structured validation errors with field-level details
Malformed JSON returns 400; unknown routes return 404 in standard error format
Durable persistence in relational database with versioned migrations
Safe concurrent updates (optimistic concurrency or row-level locking)
Max request body size enforcement; input sanitization
List responds <200ms for 100k items; supports ≥100 concurrent requests; indexed queries

components.name: todo-api
components.componentAgentInstructions:
Build a Go service using net/http on port 9090. Structure: cmd/server/main.go, internal/handlers, internal/store, internal/models, migrations/.

Persistence: Use PostgreSQL via database/sql + lib/pq (or pgx). Load config from env: DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DB_NAME, DB_SSLMODE, PORT (default 9090). For local/dev fallback, if DB env not set, may use SQLite via modernc.org/sqlite. Run embedded migrations at startup creating table:
  todos(id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, title TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, completed BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false, due_date TIMESTAMPTZ NULL, priority TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'medium', created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1)
Indexes on completed, priority, due_date, created_at.

IDs: UUIDv4 (github.com/google/uuid). Timestamps server-generated in UTC RFC3339.

Routes (all JSON, mounted under /v1 except health/ready at root and /v1):
- GET /health → 200 {"status":"ok"}
- GET /ready → 200 if DB ping ok else 503
- POST /v1/todos → validate (title required 1-200 chars, description ≤2000, priority∈{low,medium,high} default medium, due_date RFC3339). Return 201 with created entity.
- GET /v1/todos/{id} → 200 or 404
- GET /v1/todos → list with query params: limit (default 50, max 200), offset (default 0), completed (bool), priority, due_from, due_to (RFC3339), sort∈{created_at,due_date,priority}, order∈{asc,desc}. Return {items,total,limit,offset}.
- PATCH /v1/todos/{id} → partial update; bump updated_at; use optimistic concurrency via version column (increment). 409 if version mismatch when If-Match supplied; otherwise last-write-wins but safely within transaction.
- DELETE /v1/todos/{id} → 204 or 404.

Error schema: {code,message,details:[{field,message}]}. Malformed JSON → 400 code=invalid_json. Unknown route → 404 code=not_found. Validation errors → 400 code=validation_error with details. DB down → 503 code=unavailable.

Middleware: request logging (method, path, status, latency in ms) via structured logger (slog). Max body size 1MB via http.MaxBytesReader. Recover panics → 500.

Graceful shutdown: listen on SIGINT/SIGTERM, http.Server.Shutdown with 30s timeout, close DB.

Use prepared statements / parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection. Provide Dockerfile (multi-stage, distroless/alpine, non-root, exposes 9090). Include unit tests for handlers and validation, and an integration test with SQLite.
